# xtask Command Reference

<!-- Auto-generated by `xtask docs command-reference`. Do not edit manually. -->

This reference is generated from xtask's public clap command tree.
It deliberately excludes hidden automation-only plumbing that is not part of the supported operator surface.

Regenerate with `xtask docs sync` or `xtask docs command-reference`; verify drift with `xtask docs check`.

## Global Flags

| Flag | Value | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `--format` | yes | Output format (human, json, compact, silent). When omitted, auto-detects: non-TTY stdout → json, TTY → human. Explicit --format human forces human output even when stdout is redirected |
| `--json` | no | Shorthand for --format json |
| `--list-commands` | no | List all available commands and exit |
| `--bg` | no | Run command in background (returns immediately with job ID). Output is captured to files accessible via `xtask jobs` |
| `--fg` | no | Run command in foreground (default). Explicit flag for scripts |
| `-v` | no | Increase log verbosity. Use -v for INFO, -vv for DEBUG, -vvv for TRACE. Overridden by SINEX_LOG env var |

## Top-Level Commands

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `fix` | Apply automatic fixes across workspace packages |
| `check` | Run the fast workspace verification pipeline |
| `test` | Run the repo's primary nextest-backed test workflows |
| `build` | Build workspace packages while capturing compiler diagnostics |
| `work` | Execute the minimum workflow sequence to reach a target state |
| `run` | Run command for binary lifecycle management |
| `infra` | Manage local infrastructure (Postgres, NATS, VMs) |
| `jobs` | Inspect and manage background xtask jobs |
| `status` | Inspect workspace status, service health, and recent activity |
| `deps` | Analyze workspace dependency structure and impact |
| `history` | Query build, test, and runtime history recorded by xtask |
| `analytics` | `xtask analytics` — developer intelligence analytics |
| `git-stack` | Plan and materialize PR-sized git branch stacks from the current commit graph |
| `doctor` | Probe developer-environment health and deployment readiness |
| `privacy` | Run privacy-engine catalog, test, key, and config utilities |
| `schema` | Schema verification command group |
| `docs` | Generate and verify repo documentation surfaces |
| `source-units` | Source-unit manifest operations |
| `exercise` | Full surface area validation for xtask commands |
| `reset` | Reset developer state for a fresh start |

## `xtask fix`

Apply automatic fixes across workspace packages

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `-p, --package` | yes | no | Packages to fix (default: all workspace packages) |
| `-a, --all` | no | no | Fix ALL packages (disables affected mode default) |
| `-t, --thorough` | no | no | Thorough mode: iterate packages individually for maximum fix coverage. Slower but catches more fixes since clippy --fix only applies to freshly compiled code |
| `--smart` | no | no | Smart mode: only fix packages that have stored MachineApplicable diagnostics. Falls back to normal fix if no diagnostic data is available |


## `xtask check`

Run the fast workspace verification pipeline

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--lint` | no | no | Run clippy lints (slower, ~20s warm — subsumes cargo check) |
| `--fmt` | no | no | Run cargo fmt --check |
| `--forbidden` | no | no | Run forbidden pattern scan |
| `--full` | no | no | Full pipeline: fmt + clippy + forbidden (~25s warm) |
| `--fix` | no | no | Auto-fix fmt + clippy suggestions, then run full check (equivalent to: xtask fix && xtask check --full) |
| `--heavy` | no | no | Also run slow lints |
| `-a, --all` | no | no | Check ALL packages (disables affected mode default) |
| `-p, --package` | yes | no | Check specific package(s) only |
| `--skip-tests` | no | no | Skip test compilation check (faster, but may miss test errors) |
| `--lint-breakdown` | no | no | Show breakdown of warning counts by lint code (top 10) |
| `--by-file` | no | no | Show breakdown of warning counts by file path (top 20) |
| `--nix` | no | no | Run `nix flake check --no-build` (evaluation only, ~2-5s). Included in --full. Fails if `nix` is unavailable or unhealthy |


## `xtask test`

Run the repo's primary nextest-backed test workflows

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--debug` | no | no | Use debug profile (single-threaded, extended timeout) |
| `--fail-fast` | no | no | Stop on first failure (default: false, run all tests) |
| `-t, --threads` | yes | no | Number of threads (default: profile default; heavy defaults to <=4, debug: 1) |
| `-r, --retries` | yes | no | Test retries (nextest) |
| `--timeout` | yes | no | Test timeout (nextest) |
| `--prime` | no | no | Prime database before testing |
| `-l, --list` | no | no | List tests instead of running |
| `-E, --filter` | yes | no | Filter tests by name pattern (nextest -E filter) |
| `--scenario-tag` | yes | no | Run tests whose sinex_test scenario metadata includes this tag |
| `--scenario-category` | yes | no | Run tests whose sinex_test scenario metadata uses this category |
| `--scenario-lane` | yes | no | Run tests whose sinex_test scenario metadata uses this lane |
| `--list-scenarios` | no | no | List discovered sinex_test scenarios instead of running tests |
| `-p, --package` | yes | no | Run tests for specific package(s) |
| `--dry-run` | no | no | Print what would happen |
| `--skip-preflight` | no | no | Skip automatic infrastructure setup (preflight is ON by default) |
| `--include-ignored` | no | no | Include tests marked `#[ignore]` |
| `--heavy` | no | no | Run heavy/ignored tests |
| `-a, --all` | no | no | Run ALL packages (disables affected mode default) |
| `--update-snapshots` | no | no | Update insta snapshots (sets `INSTA_UPDATE=always`) |
| `args` | yes | no | Arguments passed to the test binary (not supported by nextest directly, usually) |

**Subcommands**

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `bench` | Run benchmarks with optional contract enforcement |
| `fuzz` | Run fuzz tests (requires cargo-fuzz) |
| `coverage` | Run code coverage analysis (requires cargo-llvm-cov) |
| `mutants` | Run mutation testing (requires cargo-mutants) |
| `vm` | Run exported NixOS VM flake checks |

### `xtask test bench`

Run benchmarks with optional contract enforcement

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--mode` | yes | no | Benchmark mode |
| `--profile` | yes | no | Nextest profile to use |
| `--runs` | yes | no | Number of runs per configuration |
| `--threads` | yes | no | Thread counts to test (comma-separated) |
| `--target` | yes | no | Target package(s) or "workspace" |
| `--contracts` | no | no | Enforce perf contracts from xtask/config/perf-contracts.toml |
| `--contracts-file` | yes | no | Contract file path (default: xtask/config/perf-contracts.toml) |
| `--report` | yes | no | Print summary from a stored perf report JSON |
| `--compare` | yes | no | Compare two perf reports: --compare <current> <previous> |
| `--output` | yes | no | Output directory for artifacts |
| `--history-db` | yes | no | History DB path for benchmark series |
| `--dry-run` | no | no | Dry run (compile only, no test execution) |
| `--verbose` | no | no | Verbose output |


### `xtask test fuzz`

Run fuzz tests (requires cargo-fuzz)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `target` | yes | no | Specific target to run (format: crate::target_name) |
| `--max-time` | yes | no | Maximum fuzzing time in seconds (default: 60) |
| `--jobs` | yes | no | Number of parallel fuzzing jobs |
| `--list` | no | no | List available fuzz targets instead of running |


### `xtask test coverage`

Run code coverage analysis (requires cargo-llvm-cov)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--output` | yes | no | Output directory |
| `--open` | no | no | Open HTML report in browser |
| `-p, --package` | yes | no | Specific package |
| `--html` | no | no | Generate HTML report (default) |
| `--enforce` | yes | no | Enforce minimum coverage threshold |


### `xtask test mutants`

Run mutation testing (requires cargo-mutants)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `-p, --package` | yes | no | Specific package |
| `-f, --file` | yes | no | Specific file to mutate |
| `--timeout` | yes | no | Timeout per mutant in seconds |
| `-j, --jobs` | yes | no | Number of parallel jobs |


### `xtask test vm`

Run exported NixOS VM flake checks

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--category` | yes | no | Test category: smoke, integration, performance, chaos, all |
| `--timeout` | yes | no | Timeout per test in seconds |
| `--keep-failed` | no | no | Keep failed VM derivations for inspection |
| `-l, --list` | no | no | List exported VM checks instead of running them |
| `--validate` | no | no | Validate VM scenario files without running them |
| `args` | yes | no | Specific exported VM checks to run |


## `xtask build`

Build workspace packages while capturing compiler diagnostics

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `-p, --package` | yes | no | Packages to build (default: all) |
| `-r, --release` | no | no | Build in release mode |
| `-a, --all` | no | no | Build ALL packages (disables affected mode default) |
| `--dry-run` | no | no | Print what would happen without building |


## `xtask work`

Execute the minimum workflow sequence to reach a target state

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `target` | yes | yes | Target operation to reach (check, test, build) |


## `xtask run`

Run command for binary lifecycle management

**Subcommands**

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `ingestd` | Run sinex-ingestd |
| `gateway` | Run sinex-gateway |
| `node` | Run a specific node by name |
| `core` | Run core services bundle (ingestd + gateway) |
| `all-ingestors` | Run all ingestors |
| `all-automatons` | Run all automatons |
| `list` | List available binaries |
| `tether` | Connect to a remote environment via The Tether |

### `xtask run ingestd`

Run sinex-ingestd

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--instance-id` | yes | no | Instance ID for multi-instance coordination |


### `xtask run gateway`

Run sinex-gateway

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--instance-id` | yes | no | Instance ID for multi-instance coordination |


### `xtask run node`

Run a specific node by name

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `name` | yes | yes | Node name (e.g., fs-ingestor, analytics-automaton) |
| `--instance-id` | yes | no | Instance ID for multi-instance coordination |


### `xtask run core`

Run core services bundle (ingestd + gateway)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--instance-id` | yes | no | Instance ID prefix |


### `xtask run all-ingestors`

Run all ingestors

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--instance-id` | yes | no | Instance ID prefix |


### `xtask run all-automatons`

Run all automatons

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--instance-id` | yes | no | Instance ID prefix |


### `xtask run list`

List available binaries


### `xtask run tether`

Connect to a remote environment via The Tether

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `target` | yes | yes | Target environment (e.g., "prod", "staging") |
| `--filter` | yes | no | Subject filter for events (default: events.>) |
| `--from-beginning` | no | no | Start from the beginning of the stream |
| `--from-sequence` | yes | no | Start from a specific sequence number |


## `xtask infra`

Manage local infrastructure (Postgres, NATS, VMs)

**Subcommands**

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `start` | Start the infrastructure |
| `stop` | Stop the infrastructure |
| `status` | Show infrastructure status |
| `logs` | View logs |
| `schema-apply` | Apply the declarative schema to a database |
| `tls-init-gateway` | Generate gateway TLS certificates using rcgen |
| `vm` | Manage VM integration |
| `flake-stage` | Stage a flake-safe checkout copy for local Nix builds and deploys |

### `xtask infra start`

Start the infrastructure

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--all` | no | no | Start all processes |
| `processes` | yes | no | Specific processes to start |


### `xtask infra stop`

Stop the infrastructure


### `xtask infra status`

Show infrastructure status

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `-w, --watch` | no | no | Watch mode |


### `xtask infra logs`

View logs

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `process` | yes | no | Process name |
| `-l, --lines` | yes | no | Lines to show |
| `-f, --follow` | no | no | Follow output |


### `xtask infra schema-apply`

Apply the declarative schema to a database

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--database-url` | yes | no | Target database URL. Falls back to DATABASE_URL, then the current checkout stack |


### `xtask infra tls-init-gateway`

Generate gateway TLS certificates using rcgen

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--output-dir` | yes | no | Output directory for generated files |
| `--san` | yes | no | Subject alternative name to include. Repeat for multiple SANs |
| `--ca-name` | yes | no | Common name for the generated certificate authority |
| `--validity-days` | yes | no | Certificate validity in days |
| `--force` | no | no | Overwrite an existing certificate set |


### `xtask infra vm`

Manage VM integration

**Subcommands**

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `test` | Run exported NixOS VM flake checks |
| `start` | Start an interactive VM |
| `ssh` | SSH into a running VM |
| `stop` | Stop a running VM |
| `snapshot` | Manage VM snapshots |

#### `xtask infra vm test`

Run exported NixOS VM flake checks

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `-c, --category` | yes | no | Test category: smoke, integration, performance, chaos, all |
| `-t, --timeout` | yes | no | Timeout per test in seconds (default: 900, closure-heavy scenarios: 3600) |
| `-k, --keep-failed` | no | no | Keep VM state after test failure for debugging |
| `-l, --list` | no | no | List available tests |
| `--validate` | no | no | Validate VM test infrastructure (nix syntax check) |
| `tests` | yes | no | Specific test names to run |


#### `xtask infra vm start`

Start an interactive VM

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `preset` | yes | yes | VM preset name. Interactive preset wiring is not implemented yet |
| `--persistent` | no | no | Keep state between runs |
| `--snapshot` | yes | no | Start from a snapshot |


#### `xtask infra vm ssh`

SSH into a running VM


#### `xtask infra vm stop`

Stop a running VM


#### `xtask infra vm snapshot`

Manage VM snapshots

**Subcommands**

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `create` | Create a named snapshot |
| `restore` | Restore from a snapshot |
| `list` | List available snapshots |

##### `xtask infra vm snapshot create`

Create a named snapshot

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `name` | yes | yes |  |


##### `xtask infra vm snapshot restore`

Restore from a snapshot

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `name` | yes | yes |  |


##### `xtask infra vm snapshot list`

List available snapshots


### `xtask infra flake-stage`

Stage a flake-safe checkout copy for local Nix builds and deploys

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--output-dir` | yes | no | Output directory for the staged checkout. Defaults to a unique /tmp path |
| `--force` | no | no | Replace an existing output directory instead of failing |


## `xtask jobs`

Inspect and manage background xtask jobs

**Subcommands**

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `list` | List recent jobs |
| `active` | List active jobs |
| `status` | Show status of a specific job |
| `output` | Show full output of a job |
| `wait` | Wait for a job to complete |
| `cancel` | Cancel a running job |
| `prune` | Remove completed jobs older than N days |

### `xtask jobs list`

List recent jobs

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--limit` | yes | no |  |
| `--active` | no | no | Show only running/active jobs |


### `xtask jobs active`

List active jobs

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--limit` | yes | no |  |


### `xtask jobs status`

Show status of a specific job

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | yes | yes |  |
| `-f, --follow` | no | no |  |


### `xtask jobs output`

Show full output of a job

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | yes | yes |  |
| `--stderr` | no | no |  |


### `xtask jobs wait`

Wait for a job to complete

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | yes | yes |  |
| `-t, --timeout` | yes | no |  |


### `xtask jobs cancel`

Cancel a running job

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | yes | yes |  |


### `xtask jobs prune`

Remove completed jobs older than N days

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--older-than` | yes | no |  |


## `xtask status`

Inspect workspace status, service health, and recent activity

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `-w, --watch` | no | no | Watch for changes (live updates) |
| `--summary` | no | no | Rich multi-section MOTD |
| `--schemas` | no | no | Show event payload schema information |


## `xtask deps`

Analyze workspace dependency structure and impact

**Subcommands**

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `list` | List all workspace packages |
| `tree` | Show dependency tree for a package |
| `duplicates` | Find duplicate dependencies (multiple versions) |
| `unused` | Detect unused dependencies |
| `timings` | Analyze build timings |
| `impact` | Analyze rebuild impact of package changes |
| `graph` | Visualize dependency graph |

### `xtask deps list`

List all workspace packages

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--all` | no | no | Include transitive dependencies |


### `xtask deps tree`

Show dependency tree for a package

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--package` | yes | no | Target package (defaults to all workspace packages) |
| `--depth` | yes | no | Maximum tree depth |


### `xtask deps duplicates`

Find duplicate dependencies (multiple versions)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--threshold` | yes | no | Minimum number of versions to report |


### `xtask deps unused`

Detect unused dependencies

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--ci` | no | no | Fail build if unused dependencies found (for CI) |


### `xtask deps timings`

Analyze build timings

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--compare` | yes | no | Compare with previous build |
| `--top` | yes | no | Number of slowest crates to show |


### `xtask deps impact`

Analyze rebuild impact of package changes

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--package` | yes | no | Target package to analyze (defaults to all) |


### `xtask deps graph`

Visualize dependency graph

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--render-format` | yes | no | Render format (dot, json, ascii) |
| `--focus` | yes | no | Focus on specific package |
| `--reverse` | no | no | Show reverse dependencies |
| `--depth` | yes | no | Maximum depth |
| `-o, --output` | yes | no | Output file (if not specified, writes to stdout) |


## `xtask history`

Query build, test, and runtime history recorded by xtask

**Subcommands**

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `list` | List recent invocations |
| `stats` | Show statistics for a command (or all commands / all packages) |
| `tests` | Query test result history |
| `diagnostics` | Query build diagnostics (warnings/errors) |
| `stages` | Query pipeline stage timing data (G2) |
| `fix` | Query fix session history (G3) |
| `view` | Semantic named views — shortcuts to common queries (I1) |
| `query` | Execute a read-only SQL query against the history database (I2) |
| `shell` | Open an interactive SQLite shell on the history database (I2) |
| `schema` | Dump CREATE TABLE statements for the history database schema (I2) |
| `timeline` | Cross-invocation chronological view of recent activity (I4) |
| `diff` | Compare two invocations: diagnostic delta, duration delta, stage delta (I5) |
| `sessions` | Group invocations into working sessions separated by inactivity gaps (I6) |
| `invocation` | Show complete details for a single invocation (I7) |
| `seed` | Seed the history database with synthetic data for exploration (T2) |
| `progress` | Show live or final progress for an invocation |
| `eta` | Show ETA estimates for a command based on recorded phase timings |
| `exercise` | Show exercise run history with pass/fail counts and regression detection |

### `xtask history list`

List recent invocations

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--limit` | yes | no |  |
| `--command` | yes | no |  |
| `--first` | no | no | Show only the most recent invocation (like the old `last` subcommand) |
| `--no-limit` | no | no | Export all history as JSON without limit |
| `--offset` | yes | no | Skip N entries (pagination) |
| `--after-invocation` | yes | no | Only show invocations with IDs greater than this selector (`latest`, `previous`, `current`, numeric, `inv:<id>`, `job:<id>`) |
| `--before-invocation` | yes | no | Only show invocations with IDs less than this selector (`latest`, `previous`, `current`, numeric, `inv:<id>`, `job:<id>`) |
| `--sort-by` | yes | no | Sort field: started (default), duration, status |
| `--since` | yes | no | Only show invocations started after this duration ago (e.g. "1h", "30m", "1d") |
| `--with-diagnostics` | no | no | Include diagnostic error/warning counts for each invocation |
| `--with-stages` | no | no | Include stage timing summary for each invocation |
| `--with-tests` | no | no | Include test pass/fail counts for each invocation |


### `xtask history stats`

Show statistics for a command (or all commands / all packages)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--command` | yes | no | Command to analyse (required unless --all-commands is set) |
| `--days` | yes | no |  |
| `--package` | yes | no | Narrow to a specific package (uses invocation_packages join) |
| `--all-packages` | no | no | Show stats for all packages that have appeared in diagnostics (G4) |
| `--all-commands` | no | no | Show stats for every command in the history (G4) |


### `xtask history tests`

Query test result history

**Subcommands**

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `slowest` |  |
| `flaky` |  |
| `getting-slower` |  |
| `trends` |  |
| `failures` | Show failing tests from the most recent test run |
| `analyze` | Comprehensive analysis of the most recent test run |
| `output` | Show captured output for a test (pass or fail) |
| `eta` |  |
| `grep` | Full-text search across stored test output (G7) |
| `by-package` | Per-package pass rate, test count, avg duration, and flaky count (G7) |
| `duration-p95` | P95 duration per test over recent runs (G7) |
| `regression` | Tests newly failing in the last N runs that previously passed (G7) |

#### `xtask history tests slowest`

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--limit` | yes | no |  |
| `--invocation` | yes | no | Test run selector: `latest`, `previous`, `latest-success`, `latest-failure`, invocation ID, `inv:<id>`, or `job:<id>` |


#### `xtask history tests flaky`

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--limit` | yes | no |  |


#### `xtask history tests getting-slower`

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--threshold-pct` | yes | no |  |
| `--window` | yes | no |  |
| `--limit` | yes | no |  |


#### `xtask history tests trends`

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--pattern` | yes | no |  |
| `--package` | yes | no |  |
| `--runs` | yes | no |  |


#### `xtask history tests failures`

Show failing tests from the most recent test run

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--limit` | yes | no |  |
| `--output` | no | no | Show captured failure output (can be verbose) |
| `--invocation` | yes | no | Test run selector: `latest`, `previous`, `latest-success`, `latest-failure`, invocation ID, `inv:<id>`, or `job:<id>` |


#### `xtask history tests analyze`

Comprehensive analysis of the most recent test run

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--invocation` | yes | no | Test run selector: `latest`, `previous`, `latest-success`, `latest-failure`, invocation ID, `inv:<id>`, or `job:<id>` |


#### `xtask history tests output`

Show captured output for a test (pass or fail)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `pattern` | yes | yes | Test name pattern to search for |
| `--invocation` | yes | no | Test run selector: `latest`, `previous`, `latest-success`, `latest-failure`, invocation ID, `inv:<id>`, or `job:<id>` |


#### `xtask history tests eta`


#### `xtask history tests grep`

Full-text search across stored test output (G7)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `text` | yes | yes | Text to search for in captured test output |
| `--limit` | yes | no |  |
| `--invocation` | yes | no | Test run selector: `latest`, `previous`, `latest-success`, `latest-failure`, invocation ID, `inv:<id>`, or `job:<id>` |


#### `xtask history tests by-package`

Per-package pass rate, test count, avg duration, and flaky count (G7)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--invocation` | yes | no | Test run selector: `latest`, `previous`, `latest-success`, `latest-failure`, invocation ID, `inv:<id>`, or `job:<id>` |


#### `xtask history tests duration-p95`

P95 duration per test over recent runs (G7)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--limit` | yes | no |  |


#### `xtask history tests regression`

Tests newly failing in the last N runs that previously passed (G7)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--runs` | yes | no | Number of recent invocations to search for regressions |


### `xtask history diagnostics`

Query build diagnostics (warnings/errors)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--level` | yes | no | Filter by level (error, warning) |
| `--file` | yes | no | Filter by file path pattern |
| `--command` | yes | no | Filter by command (check, build, test) |
| `--package` | yes | no | Filter by package name |
| `--scope` | yes | no | Diagnostic scope: "all" (accumulated), "latest" or an invocation ID (single run), or omit for package-scoped supersession (default) |
| `--limit` | yes | no | Maximum number of diagnostics (with --scope all) |
| `--fixable` | no | no | Show only auto-fixable diagnostics (MachineApplicable) |
| `--trend` | no | no | Show diagnostic count trend over recent invocations |
| `--window` | yes | no | Number of invocations to include in trend (with --trend) |
| `--emit` | yes | no | Output format: table (default) or gcc (file:line:col: level: message) |
| `--delta` | no | no | Show diagnostic delta between two invocations (new/resolved/persistent) |
| `--delta-from` | yes | no | Base invocation ID for delta comparison (defaults to previous check invocation) |
| `--delta-to` | yes | no | Target invocation ID for delta comparison (defaults to latest) |
| `--code` | yes | no | Filter diagnostics by error code (e.g. E0308) |
| `--by-code` | no | no | Group and summarise diagnostics by error code |
| `--lifecycle` | no | no | Show diagnostic lifecycle: New, Chronic, Recurring, Resolved (I3) |
| `--lifecycle-status` | yes | no | Filter --lifecycle results by status (new, chronic, recurring, resolved) |


### `xtask history stages`

Query pipeline stage timing data (G2)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--command` | yes | no | Filter by command (check, build, test, etc.) |
| `--invocation` | yes | no | Show timings for a specific invocation ID |
| `--slowest` | yes | no | Show the N slowest stages by average duration |
| `--trend` | yes | no | Show duration trend for a specific stage name |
| `--window` | yes | no | Number of invocations to include in trend (with --trend) |


### `xtask history fix`

Query fix session history (G3)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--sessions` | yes | no | Show the N most recent fix sessions |
| `--effectiveness` | no | no | Analyse fix effectiveness (before/after diagnostic counts) |


### `xtask history view`

Semantic named views — shortcuts to common queries (I1)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `name` | yes | no | View name to display. Omit to list all available views |


### `xtask history query`

Execute a read-only SQL query against the history database (I2)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--sql` | yes | yes | SQL SELECT statement to execute (read-only enforced via PRAGMA) |


### `xtask history shell`

Open an interactive SQLite shell on the history database (I2)


### `xtask history schema`

Dump CREATE TABLE statements for the history database schema (I2)


### `xtask history timeline`

Cross-invocation chronological view of recent activity (I4)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--command` | yes | no | Restrict to a specific command (check, test, build, …) |
| `--days` | yes | no | How many days back to show (default: 7) |
| `--limit` | yes | no | Maximum number of entries (default: 20) |


### `xtask history diff`

Compare two invocations: diagnostic delta, duration delta, stage delta (I5)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--from` | yes | no | Base invocation ID (default: previous invocation of same command) |
| `--to` | yes | no | Target invocation ID (default: most recent completed invocation) |
| `--command` | yes | no | Restrict diff to a specific command |


### `xtask history sessions`

Group invocations into working sessions separated by inactivity gaps (I6)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--limit` | yes | no | Number of sessions to show (default: 10) |
| `--gap-minutes` | yes | no | Inactivity gap in minutes that separates sessions (default: 30) |


### `xtask history invocation`

Show complete details for a single invocation (I7)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | yes | yes | Invocation selector: `latest`, `previous`, `current`, invocation ID, `inv:<id>`, or `job:<id>`. Bare numeric IDs resolve as invocation IDs first; use `job:<id>` for unambiguous background-job lookup |
| `--full` | no | no | Include full stage timing and diagnostic details |
| `--command` | yes | no | Filter selector resolution to a specific command |


### `xtask history seed`

Seed the history database with synthetic data for exploration (T2)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--days` | yes | no | Calendar days of history to generate (default: 30) |
| `--invocations` | yes | no | Total number of invocations to generate (default: 100) |


### `xtask history progress`

Show live or final progress for an invocation

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--invocation` | yes | no | Invocation selector: `current` (default), `latest`, `previous`, invocation ID, `inv:<id>`, or `job:<id>`. Bare numeric IDs resolve as invocation IDs first; use `job:<id>` for unambiguous background-job lookup |


### `xtask history eta`

Show ETA estimates for a command based on recorded phase timings

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `command` | yes | yes | Command name (e.g. "check", "test", "build") |
| `--phase` | yes | no | Phase name (e.g. "compile", "tests"). If omitted, shows all phases |
| `--window` | yes | no | Number of recent samples to use for the median estimate (default: 20) |


### `xtask history exercise`

Show exercise run history with pass/fail counts and regression detection

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--limit` | yes | no | Number of recent exercise runs to show (default: 10) |
| `--verbose` | no | no | Show individual exercise-level results (verbose) |


## `xtask analytics`

`xtask analytics` — developer intelligence analytics

**Subcommands**

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `workspace-health` | Composite workspace health score (0-100) across build, test, and velocity dimensions (J1) |
| `hotspots` | Diagnostic churn analysis — most active recurring and chronic issues (J2) |
| `reliability` | Test reliability per package — pass rates and flakiness (J3) |
| `velocity` | Build and test time trends (J4) |
| `recommend` | Actionable heuristic recommendations with exact commands to run (J5) |
| `resources` | CPU and memory usage trends across invocations (J6) |
| `stages` | Stage-level timing breakdowns aggregated across invocations (J7) |

### `xtask analytics workspace-health`

Composite workspace health score (0-100) across build, test, and velocity dimensions (J1)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--breakdown` | no | no | Show per-package breakdown |


### `xtask analytics hotspots`

Diagnostic churn analysis — most active recurring and chronic issues (J2)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--limit` | yes | no |  |


### `xtask analytics reliability`

Test reliability per package — pass rates and flakiness (J3)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--limit` | yes | no |  |


### `xtask analytics velocity`

Build and test time trends (J4)


### `xtask analytics recommend`

Actionable heuristic recommendations with exact commands to run (J5)


### `xtask analytics resources`

CPU and memory usage trends across invocations (J6)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--command` | yes | no | Filter by command (e.g., "check", "test") |
| `--limit` | yes | no | Number of recent invocations to show |


### `xtask analytics stages`

Stage-level timing breakdowns aggregated across invocations (J7)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--command` | yes | no | Filter by command |
| `--limit` | yes | no | Number of slowest stages to show |


## `xtask git-stack`

Plan and materialize PR-sized git branch stacks from the current commit graph

**Subcommands**

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `plan` | Walk the current commit graph and generate a stack plan plus PR/squash bodies |
| `materialize` | Materialize branch refs from an existing stack plan |
| `split` | Generate a stack plan and immediately materialize the resulting branches |
| `publish` | Push materialized branches and open/reuse PRs from a generated stack plan |

### `xtask git-stack plan`

Walk the current commit graph and generate a stack plan plus PR/squash bodies

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--base` | yes | no | Base ref to split against. Defaults to origin/master when available, else master |
| `--head` | yes | no | Head ref to analyze |
| `--branch-prefix` | yes | no | Branch prefix for generated slice refs |
| `--max-commits-per-slice` | yes | no | Maximum number of commits per generated slice before forcing a split |
| `--output` | yes | no | Output directory for generated artifacts |
| `--force` | no | no | Overwrite an existing output directory |


### `xtask git-stack materialize`

Materialize branch refs from an existing stack plan

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--plan` | yes | yes | Path to a generated `plan.yaml` |
| `--force` | no | no | Overwrite existing branch refs |
| `--allow-blockers` | no | no | Continue even when the plan recorded blockers |


### `xtask git-stack split`

Generate a stack plan and immediately materialize the resulting branches

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--base` | yes | no | Base ref to split against. Defaults to origin/master when available, else master |
| `--head` | yes | no | Head ref to analyze |
| `--branch-prefix` | yes | no | Branch prefix for generated slice refs |
| `--max-commits-per-slice` | yes | no | Maximum number of commits per generated slice before forcing a split |
| `--output` | yes | no | Output directory for generated artifacts |
| `--force` | no | no | Overwrite an existing output directory and existing branch refs |
| `--allow-blockers` | no | no | Continue even when the generated plan records blockers |


### `xtask git-stack publish`

Push materialized branches and open/reuse PRs from a generated stack plan

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--plan` | yes | yes | Path to a generated `plan.yaml` |
| `--remote` | yes | no | Git remote to push branch refs to |
| `--ready` | no | no | Create PRs as ready-for-review instead of drafts |
| `--push-only` | no | no | Push branches only; skip GitHub PR creation |
| `--force-with-lease` | no | no | Force-update the remote branches with `--force-with-lease` |
| `--repo` | yes | no | Optional GitHub repo override for `gh`, e.g. `owner/name` |
| `--allow-blockers` | no | no | Continue even when the plan recorded blockers |


## `xtask doctor`

Probe developer-environment health and deployment readiness

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--pipelines` | no | no | Run pipeline smoke tests in addition to health checks |
| `--fix` | no | no | Auto-remediate: restart stale processes, invalidate stale preflight cache |
| `--runtime` | no | no | Check runtime health (ingestd heartbeat, consumer lag, batch latency) |
| `--deployment-readiness` | no | no | Check deployment readiness (schema, services, permissions) |


## `xtask privacy`

Run privacy-engine catalog, test, key, and config utilities

**Subcommands**

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `catalog` | List all privacy rules in the built-in catalog |
| `test` | Test input text against the privacy engine |
| `decrypt` | Decrypt an encrypted privacy token |
| `key` | Show privacy key information |
| `config` | Show or generate privacy configuration |

### `xtask privacy catalog`

List all privacy rules in the built-in catalog

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `-c, --category` | yes | no | Filter by category (secret, pii, privacy, custom) |
| `--include-disabled` | no | no | Show disabled rules |


### `xtask privacy test`

Test input text against the privacy engine

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `input` | yes | yes | Input text to process |
| `-c, --context` | yes | no | Processing context (command, clipboard, window_title, journal, dbus, notification, document, metadata) |


### `xtask privacy decrypt`

Decrypt an encrypted privacy token

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `token` | yes | yes | The encrypted token (starts with ⌜enc:) |


### `xtask privacy key`

Show privacy key information

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--generate` | no | no | Generate a new random 256-bit key (hex-encoded) |


### `xtask privacy config`

Show or generate privacy configuration

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--init` | no | no | Generate an example TOML config file to stdout |


## `xtask schema`

Schema verification command group

**Subcommands**

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `strict-diff` | Detect strict schema drift that declarative apply does not reconcile |

### `xtask schema strict-diff`

Detect strict schema drift that declarative apply does not reconcile

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--database-url` | yes | no | Database URL to inspect. Defaults to DATABASE_URL |


## `xtask docs`

Generate and verify repo documentation surfaces

**Subcommands**

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `build` | Build documentation |
| `serve` | Serve documentation locally (requires `simple-http-server` or `python3`) |
| `agents` | Generate AGENTS.md by resolving the CLAUDE.md transclusion tree |
| `command-guide` | Generate the concise xtask command guide used for agent memory and humans |
| `command-reference` | Generate the exhaustive xtask public command reference from the live clap tree |
| `schema-bundle` | Generate the checked-in JSON schema bundle from the Rust EventPayload registry |
| `proof-catalog` | Generate the proof catalog from Rust descriptors, payloads, commands, and scenarios |
| `ast-grep-catalog` | Generate the live ast-grep rule catalog from `.config/ast-grep/rules/` |
| `sync` | Refresh all generated repo surfaces tracked in the repo |
| `check` | Verify that all generated repo surfaces are up to date |
| `snapshot` | Generate a codebase snapshot for AI context (via repomix) |
| `issue-drift` | Detect drift between GitHub issue bodies and the live workspace |

### `xtask docs build`

Build documentation

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `-p, --package` | yes | no | Build for specific package(s) |
| `--open` | no | no | Open in browser after build |
| `--private` | no | no | Include private items |
| `--all-features` | no | no | Build all-features documentation |


### `xtask docs serve`

Serve documentation locally (requires `simple-http-server` or `python3`)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `-p, --port` | yes | no | Port to serve on |
| `--build` | no | no | Build docs before serving |


### `xtask docs agents`

Generate AGENTS.md by resolving the CLAUDE.md transclusion tree

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--output` | yes | no | Output file (default: AGENTS.md in workspace root) |
| `--stdout` | no | no | Print to stdout instead of writing a file |
| `--check` | no | no | Exit non-zero if the generated output would change |


### `xtask docs command-guide`

Generate the concise xtask command guide used for agent memory and humans

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--output` | yes | no | Output file (default: xtask/docs/command-guide.md) |
| `--stdout` | no | no | Print to stdout instead of writing a file |
| `--check` | no | no | Exit non-zero if the generated output would change |


### `xtask docs command-reference`

Generate the exhaustive xtask public command reference from the live clap tree

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--output` | yes | no | Output file (default: xtask/docs/command-reference.md) |
| `--stdout` | no | no | Print to stdout instead of writing a file |
| `--check` | no | no | Exit non-zero if the generated output would change |


### `xtask docs schema-bundle`

Generate the checked-in JSON schema bundle from the Rust EventPayload registry

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--output-dir` | yes | no | Output directory root (default: schemas/ in workspace root) |
| `--check` | no | no | Exit non-zero if the generated bundle would change |


### `xtask docs proof-catalog`

Generate the proof catalog from Rust descriptors, payloads, commands, and scenarios

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--output` | yes | no | Output file (default: docs/proof-catalog.json) |
| `--stdout` | no | no | Print to stdout instead of writing a file |
| `--check` | no | no | Exit non-zero if the generated output would change |


### `xtask docs ast-grep-catalog`

Generate the live ast-grep rule catalog from `.config/ast-grep/rules/`

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--output` | yes | no | Output file (default: .config/ast-grep/README.md) |
| `--stdout` | no | no | Print to stdout instead of writing a file |
| `--check` | no | no | Exit non-zero if the generated output would change |


### `xtask docs sync`

Refresh all generated repo surfaces tracked in the repo


### `xtask docs check`

Verify that all generated repo surfaces are up to date


### `xtask docs snapshot`

Generate a codebase snapshot for AI context (via repomix)

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `-o, --output` | yes | no | Output file path |
| `--include` | yes | no | Include patterns (glob) |
| `--exclude` | yes | no | Exclude patterns (glob) |
| `--compress` | no | no | Use Tree-sitter to extract essential code structure (smaller output) |
| `--remove-comments` | no | no | Remove code comments from output |
| `--diagnostics` | no | no | U1: Auto-include files mentioned in the most recent build_diagnostics run |
| `--changed` | no | no | U2: Include files changed since HEAD (staged + unstaged) |
| `--context` | no | no | U3: Inject structured xtask state (recent checks, diagnostics, jobs) into the snapshot |
| `--project-memory` | no | no | U4: Include CLAUDE.md and .agent/includes/ (project memory) in the snapshot |
| `--scope` | yes | no | U5: Scope to a crate or directory group (e.g., sinex-db, core, nodes, tests) |


### `xtask docs issue-drift`

Detect drift between GitHub issue bodies and the live workspace

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--limit` | yes | no | Maximum number of issues to fetch (default: 500) |
| `--issue` | yes | no | Only report findings for the specified issue number |
| `--duplicate-threshold` | yes | no | Minimum shingle-similarity ratio (0.0–1.0) for duplicate detection |
| `--no-duplicates` | no | no | Skip duplicate detection (faster when corpus is large) |


## `xtask source-units`

Source-unit manifest operations

**Subcommands**

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `render` | Render the source-unit manifest |
| `check` | Validate source-unit descriptors and the generated manifest |

### `xtask source-units render`

Render the source-unit manifest

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--output` | yes | no | Output file (default: docs/source-units.json) |
| `--stdout` | no | no | Print to stdout instead of writing a file |


### `xtask source-units check`

Validate source-unit descriptors and the generated manifest

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--output` | yes | no | Manifest path to check (default: docs/source-units.json) |


## `xtask exercise`

Full surface area validation for xtask commands

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--all` | no | no | Run all tiers (default: tier 1 only) |
| `--tier` | yes | no | Specific tier(s) to run (1-4, repeatable) |
| `--id` | yes | no | Run specific exercise(s) by ID |
| `--list` | no | no | List exercises without running |
| `--dry-run` | no | no | Alias for --list |
| `--skip-infra` | no | no | Skip exercises needing infrastructure (Postgres/NATS) |
| `--verbose` | no | no | Stream exercise output in real-time |
| `--fail-fast` | no | no | Stop on first failure (default: continue all) |
| `--seed` | no | no | Seed a temporary history database before running exercises (T1) |
| `--seed-days` | yes | no | Days of history to generate for --seed (default: 30) |
| `--seed-invocations` | yes | no | Number of invocations to generate for --seed (default: 100) |
| `--activate` | no | no | After seeding, print the DB path as an export statement for shell activation (T4) |
| `--audit-file` | yes | no | Write a deterministic QA manifest (exercise IDs + pass/fail) to this path |
| `--ci-check` | no | no | Diff results against the committed baseline, fail on regressions |
| `--baseline` | yes | no | Override the baseline path used by `--ci-check` |
| `--update-baseline` | no | no | Update the committed baseline with the current run results |


## `xtask reset`

Reset developer state for a fresh start

**Arguments**

| Flag | Value | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--yes` | no | yes | Required safety guard — must be passed to confirm destructive operation |
| `--db` | no | no | Drop and recreate the database (stops Postgres, destroys all data) |
| `--nats` | no | no | Wipe NATS JetStream data (stops NATS, deletes stream data) |
| `--blobs` | no | no | Wipe the git-annex blobstore |
| `--preflight` | no | no | Wipe the entire .sinex/preflight/ directory (forces full preflight on next run) |
| `--contracts` | no | no | Delete contracts hash file (forces contract redeploy on next run, no data loss) |
| `--schema` | no | no | Delete schema apply hash file (forces schema reapply on next run, no data loss) |
| `--history` | no | no | Delete the xtask history database |
| `--seed` | no | no | When used with --history: reseed the history database with synthetic data |
| `--jobs` | no | no | Delete background job records and output files |
| `--target` | no | no | Wipe the cargo target/ directory (forces clean recompilation) |
| `--tls` | no | no | Regenerate TLS certificates |
